sourcesContent:
# composition matrix rows for classes: declarations (bare,
# named, extends, with/without bodies), methods, the constructor,
# static methods, bound (=>) methods with an explicit constructor,
# super in constructors and methods (bare and member forms), `new`
# with and without arguments, class expressions in assignment-value,
# call-argument (implicit call), array-element, and member-access
# positions, and instances driving the whole matrix at eval time.
class Animal
  constructor: (name) ->
    @name = name
  speak: -> "..."
  describe: -> "#{@name} says #{@speak()}"
  @kingdom: -> "Animalia"

class Dog extends Animal
  constructor: (name) ->
    super(name)
  speak: -> "woof"
  describe: -> super() + "!"

class Counter
  constructor: ->
    @n = 0
  tick: =>
    @n += 1
    @n

# instantiation: implicit args, explicit args, bare new
d = new Dog "Rex"
a = new Animal("Generic")
c = new Counter

# methods, inheritance, statics
s1 = d.speak()
s2 = d.describe()
s3 = a.speak()
k = Animal.kingdom()

# bound method survives detachment (the constructor binds it)
tick = c.tick
t1 = tick()
t2 = tick()

# class expressions: assignment value, implicit call argument,
# array element, member access on an instance of an expression class
Maker = class
  make: -> "made"
made = (new Maker()).make()

hold = (x) -> x
Held = hold class Token
  kind: -> "held"
h = (new Held()).kind()

pair = [class L, class R]
lname = pair[0].name

# single-line members whose bodies contain multi-argument implicit
# calls: the commas belong to the call, not the member list
add = (p, q) -> p + q
class Calc
  two: -> add 1, 1
  three: -> add 1, add 1, 1
sums = [new Calc().two(), (new Calc()).three()]
